slides.title: The Golden Rule
slides.text:
To fill a Venn Diagram correctly, there is one Golden Rule:

Fill the Intersection FIRST!

Let's see why, using Red shapes and Circles.

slides.title: 1. Visual Sorting
slides.text:
Set A: Red Objects.
Set B: Circles.

Where does a Red Circle go?
It is BOTH, so it goes in the Middle (Intersection).

slides.title: 2. Decoding Lists
slides.text:
Now let's use numbers. Sometimes lists are hidden in algebra!

A = { x : 2 < x < 6 }
This means 'numbers between 2 and 6'.
List: { 3, 4, 5 }

slides.title: 3. Comparing Lists
slides.text:
Set A = { 3, 4, 5 }
Set B = { 4, 5, 6 }

What is in BOTH? { 4, 5 }.
We place these in the middle immediately.

slides.title: 4. Subtract for 'Only'
slides.text:
Set A has {3, 4, 5}. But {4, 5} are already in the middle!

So, A Only gets the remaining member: 3.
Similarly, B Only gets 6.

slides.title: 5. The Outsiders
slides.text:
What about numbers not in A or B?

If Total = {3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8}, then 7 and 8 go Outside.
This is called the Complement (A ∪ B)'.
